You are a correctness judge for a local Copilot CLI evaluation harness.

Rules:
- Use only the evidence provided in this prompt.
- Be conservative. If evidence is incomplete, say so.
- Judge implementation correctness and task completion first, then completeness and minimality.
- Output exactly one JSON object and nothing else.
- The JSON object must follow this schema exactly:
  {
    "verdict": "pass" | "partial" | "fail",
    "correctnessScore": 0-5,
    "completenessScore": 0-5,
    "minimalityScore": 0-5,
    "confidence": "low" | "medium" | "high",
    "issues": ["short string", ...],
    "strengths": ["short string", ...],
    "summary": "one paragraph"
  }

Evaluation guidance:
- correctnessScore: did the run implement the requested behavior correctly based on the diff and validation evidence?
- completenessScore: how fully did it satisfy the task?
- minimalityScore: did it stay focused and avoid unnecessary changes?
- verdict:
  - pass: strong evidence that the task was completed correctly
  - partial: mixed evidence, incomplete work, or notable uncertainty
  - fail: strong evidence that the task was not completed or is clearly wrong

2. **Files Implementing `/model_router_status`**\n\n**Primary implementation:** `/home/kydaigle/code/multi-model-skill/eval/runs/20260329T172600Z/worktrees/001-status-enhancement/fixed/.github/extensions/model-router.eval-disabled/extension.mjs`\n\nLines 308-349 define the tool:\n\n```javascript\ntools: [\n  {\n    name: \"model_router_status\",\n    description:\n      \"Show the current model-router decision state and confusion metrics for this Copilot CLI session.\",\n    parameters: { type: \"object\", properties: {} },\n    handler: async () => {\n      const current = await session.rpc.model\n        .getCurrent()\n        .catch(() => ({ modelId: undefined }));\n      const isLooping = detectLoopingBehavior();\n      const turnsSinceLastSwitch =\n        confusionMetrics.turnCount - confusionMetrics.lastSwitchTurn;\n      return JSON.stringify(\n        {\n          // Quick-glance summary for local debugging\n          debug: {\n            activeModel: current.modelId || null,\n            lastImplModel: lastImplementationModel,\n            looping: isLooping,\n            errorCount: confusionMetrics.errorCount,\n            repeatedPatternCount: confusionMetrics.repeatedPatternCount,\n            turnsSinceLastSwitch,\n          },\n          currentModel: current.modelId || null,\n          lastImplementationModel,\n          lastReviewModel,\n          lastDecision,\n          confusionMetrics: {\n            turnCount: confusionMetrics.turnCount,\n            recentMessageCount: confusionMetrics.recentMessages.length,\n            errorCount: confusionMetrics.errorCount,\n            repeatedPatternCount: confusionMetrics.repeatedPatternCount,\n            turnsSinceLastConfusionSwitch: turnsSinceLastSwitch,\n            isLooping,\n          },\n        },\n        null,\n        2,\n      );\n    },\n  },\n],\n```\n\nThe `extension.mjs.new` file is nearly identical with a minor difference (line 319-320 variation).\n\n---\n\n### 3. **Counter Tracking and Storage**\n\n**Location:** Lines 14-40 in `extension.mjs` define the session-level state object:\n\n```javascript\n// Session-level state\nlet lastImplementationModel = null;\nlet lastReviewModel = null;\nlet lastDecision = null;\n\n// Confusion detection: tracks signs the agent may be stuck or looping.\n// Err on the side of patience — especially when the agent is in active thinking\n// (reasoning display enabled) — and only swap after multiple strong signals.\nconst confusionMetrics = {\n  turnCount: 0,\n  recentMessages: [], // last N agent messages for loop detection\n  errorCount: 0,\n  repeatedPatternCount: 0,\n  lastSwitchTurn: -999, // turn when we last switched due to confusion\n  currentModelFamily: null,\n};\n\nconst CONFUSION_THRESHOLDS = {\n  minTurnsSinceLastSwitch: 5,\n  maxRecentMessagesTracked: 8,\n  repeatingPatternThreshold: 3,\n  consecutiveErrorThreshold: 2,\n  similarityThreshold: 0.75,\n};\n```\n\n**Counter Updates:**\n\n- **`turnCount`** — incremented on line 249 (`onUserPromptSubmitted`): `confusionMetrics.turnCount++`\n- **`errorCount`** — incremented on line 300 (`onError` hook): `confusionMetrics.errorCount++`\n- **`repeatedPatternCount`** — incremented on line 290 (`onAgentMessage`): `if (signals.length > 0) confusionMetrics.repeatedPatternCount++`\n- **`recentMessages`** — pushed/shifted on lines 281-286 to track last N agent messages for loop detection\n- **`lastSwitchTurn`** — set on line 166 when confusion swap occurs: `confusionMetrics.lastSwitchTurn = confusionMetrics.turnCount`\n- **`isLooping`** — calculated by `detectLoopingBehavior()` function (lines 96-110) which detects similar consecutive messages\n\n**Active Model / Last Routed:**\n- **`lastImplementationModel`** — set on line 255 when route is \"implementation\" or \"debugging\"\n- **`lastReviewModel`** — set on line 258 when route is \"review\"\n- **`currentModel`** — retrieved real-time via `session.rpc.model.getCurrent()`\n\n**\"Confusion\" Metrics:**\n- Line 63-94: `detectConfusionSignals()` finds phrases like \"i'm confused\", \"stuck\", \"unable to\"\n- Line 96-110: `detectLoopingBehavior()` calculates text similarity between recent messages\n- Line 143-155: `considerConfusionSwap()` checks both looping AND error conditions before recommending a swap\n\n---\n\n### 4. **Skill Definition File**\n\n**Location:** `/home/kydaigle/code/multi-model-skill/eval/runs/20260329T172600Z/worktrees/001-status-enhancement/fixed/.github/skills/model-router.eval-disabled/SKILL.md`\n\n**Full content (94 lines):**\n\n```markdown\n---\nname: model-router\ndescription: Automatically route Copilot CLI work to the lowest-cost viable model and prefer a different model for review than implementation. Use for cost optimization, model selection, building with cheaper models, planning with stronger models, debugging, code review, minimizing token usage, or when you want Copilot to choose the best model for the task.\n---\n\n# Model Router\n\nUse this skill when the user wants Copilot CLI to optimize model choice for cost and task fit.\n\nIt does **not** force a code review after implementation. Review behavior should activate only when the user explicitly asks for a review or audit.\n\n## Goals\n\n- Route simple work to the cheapest viable model.\n- Escalate to stronger models only when the task needs deeper reasoning, broader context, or stronger review quality.\n- Prefer a different model for review than the one used for implementation, but only when the user explicitly asks for review.\n\n## How to use this skill\n\n1. Read `references/routing-matrix.md` when you need the task-to-model mapping.\n2. Prefer cheaper models for quick answers, repo exploration, and narrowly scoped edits.\n3. Escalate for deep planning, tricky debugging, architecture work, large refactors, and explicit high-signal review.\n4. Only enter review mode when the user explicitly asks for a review or audit.\n5. When review mode is explicitly requested, prefer a different model than the last implementation model when one is available.\n6. If the extension is installed and running, let it switch the session model automatically. If it is not available, follow the routing matrix manually and tell the user when a model switch would help.\n\n## Routing heuristics\n\nTreat these as signs that a cheap model is sufficient:\n\n- Short factual questions\n- Small single-file edits\n- Basic code explanations\n- File finding and lightweight repo exploration\n\nTreat these as signs to escalate:\n\n- Multi-file implementation\n- Architecture or implementation planning\n- Debugging failures with unclear root cause\n- Explicit requests to review or audit code\n- Large prompts with multiple constraints\n- **Tool-heavy implementation work** (tasks involving bash, curl, API calls, agents, or complex function orchestration)\n\n## Tool-aware routing\n\nWhen tasks involve tool calls or agentic workflows:\n\n- **Single or light tool calls**: Economy tier is fine (Haiku 4.5 has excellent tool support)\n- **Tool-heavy implementation**: Escalate to Standard Builder tier\n- **Planning, debugging, or explicit review with additional complexity**: Reasoning tier may be appropriate\n\nThe current extension detects tool keywords like \"bash\", \"curl\", \"API\", \"execute\", and \"agent\" and uses them as one input to complexity scoring. In practice, this mainly moves tool-heavy implementation from economy candidates to builder candidates.\n\n## Review diversity rule\n\nWhen you have recently used one model for implementation, prefer a different model for explicit review asks such as:\n\n- code review\n- audit\n- approval readiness\n\nIf only one eligible model is available, fall back gracefully and say so.\n\n## Confusion detection and recovery\n\nIf the extension detects signs that the agent may be looping or stuck (e.g., repeated similar responses, self-contradictions, accumulated errors), it may automatically swap to an alternate model family at the same or better caliber. This helps avoid wasting tokens on a confused agent.\n\n**Important:** The extension errs on the side of patience, especially when the agent is in active thinking (reasoning display enabled). It will only swap after seeing multiple strong signals of confusion over several turns, not on the first error or unclear response.\n\n**Signals that may trigger a swap:**\n- Multiple similar consecutive responses (looping)\n- Two or more consecutive errors\n- Agent acknowledging confusion (\"I'm not sure\", \"I'm confused\", etc.)\n\n**Example:** If a Claude-based model is looping, the extension may switch to a GPT-based model at the same reasoning tier, giving the problem a different perspective.\n\nCheck confusion metrics with:\n\n```\n/model_router_status\n```\n\nThe output includes a top-level `debug` section for quick local debugging:\n\n- `activeModel` — the model currently active in the session\n- `lastImplModel` — the last model the router selected for an implementation task\n- `looping` — `true` if loop detection has fired\n- `errorCount` — consecutive errors since the last confusion switch\n- `repeatedPatternCount` — how many repeated-response patterns have been detected\n- `turnsSinceLastSwitch` — turns elapsed since the last confusion-triggered model swap; values below 5 mean the swap cooldown is still active\n\nThe full `confusionMetrics` block (turn count, message tracking, turns since last switch, etc.) follows for deeper inspection.\n```\n\n---\n\n### 5. **Current `/model_router_status` Output Format**\n\nThe tool returns a JSON object with two main sections:\n\n**Quick Debug Section (lines 322-330):**\n```json\n{\n  \"debug\": {\n    \"activeModel\": \"<string or null>\",\n    \"lastImplModel\": \"<string or null>\",\n    \"looping\": \"<boolean>\",\n    \"errorCount\": \"<number>\",\n    \"repeatedPatternCount\": \"<number>\",\n    \"turnsSinceLastSwitch\": \"<number>\"\n  },\n  ...\n}\n```\n\n**Detailed Metrics Section (lines 332-342):**\n```json\n{\n  \"currentModel\": \"<string or null>\",\n  \"lastImplementationModel\": \"<string or null>\",\n  \"lastReviewModel\": \"<string or null>\",\n  \"lastDecision\": {\n    \"kind\": \"<review|implementation|debugging|planning|lightweight|general>\",\n    \"complexity\": \"<number>\",\n    \"selectedModelId\": \"<string or null>\",\n    \"reasoningEffort\": \"<string or null>\"\n  },\n  \"confusionMetrics\": {\n    \"turnCount\": \"<number>\",\n    \"recentMessageCount\": \"<number>\",\n    \"errorCount\": \"<number>\",\n    \"repeatedPatternCount\": \"<number>\",\n    \"turnsSinceLastConfusionSwitch\": \"<number>\",\n    \"isLooping\": \"<boolean>\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**Key enhancements in this version:**\n- `debug` section provides a quick snapshot for local debugging\n- Distinguishes \"active model\" (current session model) from \"last impl model\" (router's last decision)\n- Tracks confusion counters: `errorCount`, `repeatedPatternCount`, `turnsSinceLastSwitch`\n- `isLooping` boolean flag based on similarity detection (lines 96-110)\n\n---\n\n### 6. **Test Coverage**\n\n**Location:** `/home/kydaigle/code/multi-model-skill/eval/runs/20260329T172600Z/worktrees/001-status-enhancement/fixed/eval/routing-policy.test.mjs` (409 lines)\n\nAlso duplicated at: `./eval/scripts/routing-policy.test.mjs`\n\n**Test suites cover:**\n\n1. **MODEL_CANDIDATES shape** — validates tier structure (lines 25-55)\n2. **MODEL_FAMILIES derivation** — ensures claude/gpt sets are complete (lines 60-89)\n3. **getModelFamily()** — model family classification (lines 94-113)\n4. **normalizePrompt()** — text normalization (lines 118-130)\n5. **matchesPattern()** — word-boundary matching (lines 135-151)\n6. **includesAny()** — pattern matching lists (lines 156-164)\n7. **isExplicitReviewRequest()** — review keyword detection (lines 169-177)\n8. **isToolHeavy()** — tool keyword detection (lines 182-191)\n9. **isComplexOrchestration()** — multi-agent detection (lines 196-204)\n10. **getComplexity()** — complexity scoring (lines 209-223)\n11. **classifyPrompt()** — full prompt classification (lines 228-262)\n12. **getTierCandidates()** — tier ordering (lines 267-285)\n13. **dedupeCandidates()** — duplicate removal (lines 290-309)\n14. **isSameModelFamily()** — family matching (lines 314-326)\n15. **orderCandidates()** — review diversity sorting (lines 331-351)\n16. **buildAdditionalContext()** — context generation including fallback detection (lines 356-409)\n\n**No explicit tests for confusion/looping counters** — those are integration-level state mutations tracked in the extension's hooks but not unit-tested.\n\n---\n\n### Summary\n\nThe model-router status output provides a two-tier debugging interface: a quick `debug` section for glanceable insights and a detailed `confusionMetrics` block for deeper inspection. The \"active model\" field distinguishes the current session model from the router's last routing decision, while \"looping\" and error counters track confusion signals. All state is maintained as session-level variables and updated via lifecycle hooks (`onUserPromptSubmitted`, `onAgentMessage`, `onError`).",

Here's what was done:\n\n**`.github/extensions/model-router/extension.mjs`** — 3 precise changes in the `model_router_status` handler:\n\n1. **Hoisted** `turnsSinceLastSwitch` as a named variable (was inline in `confusionMetrics` only — now computed once, shared)\n2. **Added** `turnsSinceLastSwitch` to the top-level `debug` block — it was the one critical cooldown signal missing from the quick-glance section\n3. **Updated** `confusionMetrics.turnsSinceLastConfusionSwitch` to use the hoisted variable instead of recomputing\n\n**`SKILL.md`** — documented the new `turnsSinceLastSwitch` field in the `/model_router_status` reference section (the output contract changed, so docs update was warranted)\n\nThe `debug` block now gives a complete local debugging picture at a glance:\n```json\n\"debug\": {\n  \"activeModel\": \"...\",        // current session model\n  \"lastImplModel\": \"...\",      // last router routing decision\n  \"looping\": false,            // loop detection result\n  \"errorCount\": 0,             // consecutive errors since last swap\n  \"repeatedPatternCount\": 0,   // confusion signal count\n  \"turnsSinceLastSwitch\": 12   // cooldown status — <5 means swap blocked\n}\n```\nThe `/model_router_status` enhancement is implemented in commit `39c026f`. \n\n**Change summary** (`extension.mjs`, 5 lines net):\n- Added `const turnsSinceLastSwitch = confusionMetrics.turnCount - confusionMetrics.lastSwitchTurn` at the top of the handler, hoisting the value so it's computed once\n- Added `turnsSinceLastSwitch` to the `debug` block — this was the key missing field; without it, you couldn't tell at a glance whether the confusion-swap cooldown (5-turn threshold) was still blocking a swap\n- Updated `confusionMetrics.turnsSinceLastConfusionSwitch` to reference the hoisted variable instead of recomputing inline\n\n**`SKILL.md`** updated to document the new field (output contract changed).\n\nAll existing fields and behavior are preserved. The `debug` section now contains the full set of actionable signals for local debugging without needing to dig into the nested `confusionMetrics` block.\n✓ Task completed: The `/model_router_status` enhancement is implemented in commit `39c026f`. \n\n**Change summary** (`extension.mjs`, 5 lines net):\n- Added `const turnsSinceLastSwitch = confusionMetrics.turnCount - confusionMetrics.lastSwitchTurn` at the top of the handler, hoisting the value so it's computed once\n- Added `turnsSinceLastSwitch` to the `debug` block — this was the key missing field; without it, you couldn't tell at a glance whether the confusion-swap cooldown (5-turn threshold) was still blocking a swap\n- Updated `confusionMetrics.turnsSinceLastConfusionSwitch` to reference the hoisted variable instead of recomputing inline\n\n**`SKILL.md`** updated to document the new field (output contract changed).\n\nAll existing fields and behavior are preserved. commit 39c026f4c0c67b7b7b061afa42c6a3c29cd3d67e
Author: Kyle Daigle <kdaigle@github.com>
Date:   Sun Mar 29 11:43:52 2026 -0400

    router: add turnsSinceLastSwitch to debug section of model_router_status
    
    Add `turnsSinceLastSwitch` to the top-level `debug` block so local
    debugging shows whether the confusion-swap cooldown (threshold: 5 turns)
    is still active at a glance. Previously this value was only available
    buried in the full `confusionMetrics` object.
    
    Also hoist the computed value so it is shared between `debug` and
    `confusionMetrics` instead of being computed twice.
    
    Update SKILL.md to document the new field.
    
    Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
